question: Series FURIA Rounds Handicap (-12.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between FURIA and 9INE in the BLAST Bounty Qualifier, scheduled for January 16 at 12:30PM ET.

If FURIA defeats 9INE by 13 or more rounds across all Matchs in this match, this market will resolve to "FURIA".

Otherwise, it will resolve to "9INE".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: Counter-Strike: FURIA vs 9INE (BO3) - BLAST Bounty Qualifier
events.description:
This market will resolve to "FURIA" if FURIA win against 9INE in the named Counter-Strike match in the BLAST Bounty Qualifier, scheduled for January 16 at 12:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "9INE" if 9INE win against FURIA in the Counter-Strike match.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

events.title: Australian Open Men's: Billy Harris vs Chris Rodesch

description:

events.title: Adelaide International: Victoria Mboko vs Anna Kalinskaya
events.description:

question: Series Gentle Mates Rounds Handicap (-2.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Gentle Mates and M80 in the BLAST Bounty Qualifier, scheduled for January 15 at 6:00AM ET.

If Gentle Mates defeats M80 by 3 or more rounds across all Matchs in this match, this market will resolve to "Gentle Mates".

Otherwise, it will resolve to "M80".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Gentle Mates vs M80 (BO3) - BLAST Bounty Qualifier
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Gentle Mates" if Gentle Mates win against M80 in the named Counter-Strike match in the BLAST Bounty Qualifier, scheduled for January 15 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "M80" if M80 win against Gentle Mates in the Counter-Strike match.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

question: Adelaide International: Reilly Opelka vs Tommy Paul
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Reilly Opelka and Tommy Paul in the Adelaide International, scheduled for January 13 at 6:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Opelka' if Reilly Opelka advances against Tommy Paul.

This market will resolve to 'Paul' if Tommy Paul advances against Reilly Opelka.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to "Over" if CA Boca Juniors and CD Riestra combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaprofesional.ar. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

question: CA Boca Juniors vs. CD Riestra: Both Teams to Score
description:
In the upcoming Primera División Argentina game between CA Boca Juniors and CD Riestra, scheduled for January 25 at 4:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both CA Boca Juniors and CD Riestra each score at least one goal during the game.

question: ASB Classic: Jenson Brooksby vs Sebastian Baez
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Jenson Brooksby and Sebastian Baez in the ASB Classic, scheduled for January 13 at 4:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Brooksby' if Jenson Brooksby advances against Sebastian Baez.

This market will resolve to 'Baez' if Sebastian Baez advances against Jenson Brooksby.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will the highest temperature in Seoul be -4°C or below on January 14?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 Jan '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

question: Map Handicap: NIP (-1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Liquid and NIP in the BLAST Bounty Qualifier, scheduled for January 15 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "NIP" if NIP wins 2 or more maps than Liquid in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Liquid".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Liquid vs NIP (BO3) - BLAST Bounty Qualifier
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Liquid" if Liquid win against NIP in the named Counter-Strike match in the BLAST Bounty Qualifier, scheduled for January 15 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "NIP" if NIP win against Liquid in the Counter-Strike match.

question: Munar vs. Cerundolo: Total Sets O/U 2.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Jaume Munar and Francisco Cerundolo in the Adelaide International, scheduled for January 13 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP statistics.
groupItemTitle: Adelaide International: Jaume Munar vs Francisco Cerundolo Total Sets: O/U 2.5

events.title: Adelaide International: Jaume Munar vs Francisco Cerundolo
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Jaume Munar and Francisco Cerundolo in the Adelaide International, scheduled for January 13 at 6:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Munar' if Jaume Munar advances against Francisco Cerundolo.

This market will resolve to 'Cerundolo' if Francisco Cerundolo advances against Jaume Munar.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
